content.problemStatement:
Hussain really likes problems that involve summation over X, where X can be subsets, paths in a tree, intervals of numbers etc.

You are given two integer sequences A1,A2,A3,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, A_3, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,A3‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč and B1,B2,B3,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, B_3, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,B3‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äč. Consider an arbitrary non-empty subset SSS of the set {1,2,‚Ä¶,N}\{1, 2, \ldots, N\}{1,2,‚Ä¶,N}. Let's define the swagness of such a set as
(max(p‚ąąS)‚ÄÖ‚ÄäAp)‚čÖ(max(p‚ąąS)‚ÄÖ‚ÄäBp)‚ÄČ.\left(\mathrm{max}_{(p \in S)} \; A_p\right) \cdot \left(\mathrm{max}_{(p \in S)} \; B_p\right) \,.(max(p‚ąąS)‚ÄčAp‚Äč)‚čÖ(max(p‚ąąS)‚ÄčBp‚Äč).

Hussain is interested in the sum of swagnesses of all possible sets SSS. (Note that there are 2N‚ąí12^N-12N‚ąí1 such sets.) Since the sum may be very large, compute it modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
constraints: 1‚Č§Ai,Bi‚Č§1061 \le A_i, B_i \le 10^61‚Č§Ai‚Äč,Bi‚Äč‚Č§106 for each valid iii
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,A3,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, A_3, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,A3‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,B3,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, B_3, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,B3‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚ÄĒ the total swagness modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
